articles.title: Police identify 17-year-old brothers as suspects in fatal Brampton shooting
articles.summary: Police in Peel Region have identified two 17-year-olds as suspects in the fatal shooting of a man in Brampton earlier this month. Investigators say that just after 3:30 p.m. on July 5, an altercation between several people in the area of Edenbrook Hill Drive and Earlsbridge Boulevard resulted in shots being fired. When police arrived, […]

articles.title: Ajax man charged with sexual assault in Whitby
articles.summary: A 33-year-old Ajax man is facing several charges after a woman claims she was sexually assaulted in Whitby. Police in Durham Region say that just after 10 p.m. on July 13, the woman was leaving Iroquois Beach Park and was walking along the sand path that runs north-west toward Gordon Street when she was forced […]

articles.title: Metrolinx cleaning chemical spill in east-end creek related to Scarborough subway extension work
articles.summary: City says it was notified of spill on July 8. Metrolinx says the spill is contained and a specialized team is working to remove "polymer-based slurry" from creek.

articles.title: Man, 21, facing almost a dozen gun-related charges, second suspect sought
articles.summary: A 21-year-old Toronto man is facing almost a dozen gun-related charges, and police say they are searching for a second person in connection with the investigation. Investigators say two men were walking in the Driftwood Avenue and Finch Avenue West area around 9:30 p.m. on July 15 when they spotted a police vehicle and attempted […]

articles.title: Man critically injured in Mississauga stabbing; 17-year-old in custody
articles.summary: A 17-year-old boy is facing an attempted murder charge after a man was critically injured following a stabbing in Mississauga. Police in Peel Region say a dispute between two people in a home in the Indian Grove and Indian Road area escalated into a violent altercation around 3 p.m. on Friday. “Officers arrived on scene […]

articles.title: Mosquitoes in Toronto, York and Halton test positive for West Nile virus
articles.summary: A batch of mosquitoes in Toronto has tested positive for West Nile virus, Toronto Public Health says.

articles.title: Body of Brampton man found in Stoney Creek culvert, police say
articles.summary: Hamilton police have identified a body found this week in a culvert in Stoney Creek, Ont., as a Brampton man who had also lived in Halifax. Taranpreet Singh Sidhu, 29, had immigrated to Canada from India in 2022. Anyone who had contact with him before his death are asked to contact police.
